tempdoc559: Elevated by tempdoc 559 Authority IV: the register is now the single authority for which EVIDENCE records exist and who projects them — SearchTrace (search-ranking 'why') and ContextCitation (RAG-retrieval citations) are sibling registered records (see siblingRecords). They share no field and never co-occur (the RAG ask flow emits no trace), so they are NOT one mega-record; the one authority is one level up — 'what evidence exists + who renders it'. The FE citation UI projects a shared evidence view-model (EvidenceItem / evidenceProjection.ts) with a labeled score; the ranking 'why' projects SearchTrace. A new unaligned *Citation referencer now fails this same gate.
note: The execution-surface register (tempdoc 553 pillar c). The SINGLE allowlist of code that may describe 'what the search pipeline did' OR carry/project retrieval evidence (559 Authority IV). Every surface here is a governed projection/consumer of one of the registered records; it is NOT permitted to model execution/evidence independently. New production code that references the canonical SearchTrace type OR the ContextCitation/RetrievalCitation evidence record MUST be added here, or the `execution-surface` gate fails the build. This file is also the discovery oracle: a new agent consults it to find where the trace/evidence is already touched (do not re-author — project). HONEST LIMIT (553 §5): the gate guards this DECLARED seam (canonical-type usage + the named guards); it cannot detect an undeclared fork that re-models execution from scratch without referencing a registered type. That residue is for review + the Phase-3 discovery checkpoint.

canonicalRecord.name: SearchTrace
canonicalRecord.note: The canonical record is rooted in the 517 (SearchDecision, SearchOutcome) value model and surfaced as the app-api SearchTrace record + the ipc-common gRPC proto. Both representations are themselves projections composed from the value model; everything else projects from one of these.

siblingRecords.name: ContextCitation
siblingRecords.note: RAG-retrieval evidence — the citations behind a generated answer (parentDocId, score, excerpt, startLine/endLine/headingText). The sibling of SearchTrace (559 Authority IV): a SECOND evidence record, NOT a SearchTrace projection (no shared field; the RAG ask flow emits no trace). Registered here so it cannot drift unregistered. PRODUCER-OWNED BOUNDARY (559 §5, cross-process follow-up): the excerpt is a backend character window (startChar/endChar) that clips mid-word; the boundary-aware fields (startLine/endLine/headingText) already ride alongside and should drive a clean window at the Worker that mints the record — the FE never re-windows. Logged in docs/observations.md.

scan.comment: The gate auto-detects undeclared surfaces by scanning these roots for references to the canonical type. Only import-detectable languages (Java/TS) are auto-scanned; proto / Python / OTel-span surfaces are import-invisible and are declared explicitly below + guarded by their named guards.
scan.executionSpanNameNote: 553 Phase B Check 5: any Java-main file emitting a search/* span-name literal must be a registered surface (catches the span-tree fork class that doesn't import the canonical type). String-literal heuristic — §5 honest limit.
scan.expectedMinPopulationNote: tempdoc 576 §5 vacuous-pass guard: the auto-scan must detect >= this many canonical-type-referencing files, else a renamed scan root would let every coverage check pass vacuously. Live count is 26 (2026-06-15); floor 12 is a conservative ~half-headroom — catches a renamed-root collapse toward zero, not normal churn. Lower this with any change that legitimately shrinks the population.

553 Phase 4a (SHIPPED): projects the canonical trace onto the root `search` OTel span as record-derived attributes — the first span-as-projection convergence step. Applied in KnowledgeHttpApiAdapter.search().

553 Phase 4a (worker): projects the worker trace slice onto the active worker span (record-derived attributes), applied in SearchResponseBuilder. The worker telemetry now projects the record, not authoring independently.

553 Phase A (SHIPPED): the worker search/* tree is now per-leg OpenInference RETRIEVER/RERANKER/CHAIN spans whose document payload (retrieval.documents.* / reranker.output_documents.* incl. content) is projected from the leg/fusion result by OpenInferenceSpanProjection — a single pure deriver, not hand-authored. Guarded by the pure-helper test + the live recording-tracer assertion in SearchExecutorOtelTopologyTest. Privacy contract amended (owner decision) to allow per-document id/score/content on spans.

553 Phase D (head, SHIPPED): the search/rerank span is now an OpenInference RERANKER projecting the CE-scored output (texts + scores) via the SHARED OpenInferenceSpans projector in telemetry — the same projector the worker search/* spans (otel-spans-worker-structure) and the head search/cross_encoder + search/lambdamart spans (in head-composer) use, so the OpenInference vocabulary lives in one place (no per-module fork). Conformance: OpenInferenceSpansProjectionTest pins the shared projector.

Emits the root `search` span (projected from the canonical trace via SearchTraceSpanProjection.attributesOf) + the head search/lambdamart + search/cross_encoder OpenInference spans (via the shared OpenInferenceSpans projector). Was the SearchExecutor->KnowledgeSearchEngine rename target (556, b463e741c); registered here in 559 since that rename moved the head span emission off the previously-declared surface (the head-composer note still references these spans). Pre-existing register drift, logged in observations.
